channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: A Jesuit priest prefers prison over a fine to draw attention to climate change
item.description: A Jesuit priest says he will rather go to prison than pay a fine of 500 euros or $541 for participating in a climate activists’ street blockade in the southern German city of Nuremberg.
item.dc:creator: Kirsten Grieshaber, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: A Palestinian from the West Bank is first detainee under 18 to die in Israeli prison, officials say
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — A teenager from the West Bank who was held in an Israeli prison for six months without being charged died after collapsing in unclear circumstances, becoming the first Palestinian under 18 to die in Israeli detention, officials said.
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Musk slams Le Pen ruling, says it will 'backfire' like Trump's as some on global right face legal troubles
item.description: Marine Le Pen’s sentencing in France has drawn global attention, with parallels made to legal battles faced by President Donald Trump, Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, and former Brazilian President Jair Bolsonaro, among other right-wing politicians.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bangkok reviews construction safety after a high-rise was crumpled by an earthquake 800 miles away
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Thai authorities said Monday they are investigating why a single office tower block under construction in Bangkok collapsed during Friday's earthquake that otherwise caused limited damage in the capital. Bangkok Gov.
item.dc:creator: JERRY HARMER, JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI and ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: What to know about the ruling barring Marine Le Pen from seeking public office for five years
item.description: PARIS (AP) — French far-right leader Marine Le Pen has been sentenced to a five-year ban on running for public office for embezzling EU funds, a major political earthquake. The ruling, which takes effect immediately, is likely to bar her from running in France’s next presidential election.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Chinese military launches large-scale drills around Taiwan
item.description: The Chinese military has announced large-scale drills around Taiwan as it again warned the self-ruled island against seeking formal independence.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Israel strikes a building in southern Beirut, killing at least 4 people
item.description: The Israeli military says it struck a building in Beirut's southern suburbs, killing at least four people.
item.dc:creator: Kareem Chehayeb, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Venezuela will have its first female saint after approval from Pope Francis
item.description: CARACAS, Venezuela (AP) — Venezuela is set to have its first female saint following approval from Pope Francis.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Finnish president: Trump should give Putin 3 weeks to agree Ukraine ceasefire
item.description: Finnish President Alexander Stubb told Fox News that President Trump should impose an April 20 deadline for his Russian counterpart, Vladimir Putin, to agree a ceasefire in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hamas terrorists torture protester to death in Gaza, leave his body on family’s doorstep: report
item.description: A Palestinian protester opposed to Hamas' rule over the Gaza Strip was beaten and tortured to death following a demonstration last week, according to reports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Greenland's new prime minister: U.S. 'will not get' island
item.description: Greenland's new Prime Minister Jens-Frederik Nielsen has called out President Trump's claims that the U.S. will "get Greenland 100 percent" and has reiterated that Greenland will decide its own future and will not be shaken by Trump's remarks.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, The Hill

item.title: Greenland's prime minister says US will not 'get' island
item.description: Jens-Frederik Nielsen, Greenland's prime minister, said in a Facebook post on Sunday that the United States will not be able to annex the resource-rich island in the Atlantic.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump says ‘terrorists who attacked the beautiful Turnberry’ have been captured
item.description: President Trump said he was informed that the “terrorists who attacked" his Turnberry golf resort in Scotland had been captured.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: French court finds right-wing leader Marine Le Pen guilty of embezzlement
item.description: PARIS (AP) — A French court found Marine Le Pen guilty on Monday in an embezzlement case but didn’t immediately say what her sentence might be and how it might impact the right-wing leader’s political future.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Greenland prime minister says US will not get Greenland
item.description: The prime minister of Greenland pushed back Sunday against assertions by U.S. President Donald Trump that America will take control of the island territory. Greenland, a huge, resource-rich island in the Atlantic, is a self-governing territory of Denmark, a NATO ally of the United States.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Chair of African charity Prince Harry co-founded says the royal orchestrated a bullying campaign
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The chairperson of an African charity co-founded by Prince Harry accused the royal on Sunday of orchestrating a bullying and harassment campaign to try to force her out as she pushed back following his abrupt resignation from the organization.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MELLEY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Smell of death permeates Myanmar cities after quake kills over 1,600 and leaves countless buried
item.description: MANDALAY, Myanmar (AP) — The smell of decaying bodies permeated the streets of Myanmar's second-largest city on Sunday as people worked frantically by hand to clear rubble in the hope of finding someone still alive, two days after a massive earthquake struck that killed more than 1,600 people and le
item.dc:creator: THEIN ZAW, DAVID RISING and GRANT PECK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump threatens to bomb Iran unless they end nuclear weapons program and begin talks on new deal
item.description: Trump warned Iran after sending a letter to revive nuclear talks. With "maximum pressure" back on the table, tensions between the two nations continue to rise.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Landmark UK report on Hamas massacre exposes worst attack on Jews since Holocaust
item.description: A UK Parliament report chaired by respected historian Lord Andrew Roberts calls the Oct. 7 Hamas attack in Israel deliberate and brutal, urging democracies to combat denial and misinformation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Officials survey damage in Myanmar's earthquake-devastated central areas
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Friday’s deadly earthquake rattled most of Myanmar and Thailand but certain areas sustained the heaviest damage, including flattened buildings and many lost lives. The death toll of the 7.7 magnitude earthquake on Friday rose quickly in Myanmar and was at 1,644 victims by Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hegseth announces plan for US command upgrades in Japan to deter China
item.description: U.S. Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth announced a plan to upgrade the U.S. military command in Japan, an "indispensable" country against Chinese aggression.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Myanmar quake death toll rises to 1,644 as resistance movement announces partial ceasefire
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — A unilateral partial ceasefire to facilitate earthquake relief efforts was announced on Saturday by Myanmar’s shadow National Unity Government, which coordinates the popular struggle against the ruling military. The country's death toll from the disaster soared to 1,644.
item.dc:creator: GRANT PECK and JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: With federal election looming, Liberal Canadian PM accused of plagiarism while studying at Oxford: report
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney is embroiled in controversy after a Canadian news outlet discovered at least 10 instances of possible plagiarism in his 1995 Oxford thesis.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Latest: Countries sending humanitarian aid after Myanmar earthquake
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — The death toll from Myanmar's powerful 7.7 magnitude earthquake keeps climbing amid rescue efforts. The military government said Saturday that 1,644 people have been killed, with thousands of others injured and dozens missing.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Countries that have issued travel advisories for US
item.description: Denmark, Finland, France and Germany noted the U.S. requires individuals entering to choose "male" or "female" on ESTA and visa applications.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Bali expands tourist guidelines over misbehavior from travelers
item.description: Rules include shelling out Bali's tourist levy — equivalent to $9 USD — and wearing "modest and respectful clothing" in public.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Missing US soldiers in Lithuania killed: NATO chief
item.description: Four U.S. soldiers who went missing while training in Lithuania have died, NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte said Wednesday. Speaking to reporters during a trip to Warsaw, Rutte said he had received word of the deaths of the four American troops.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, NewsNation
